_comment_overview: ConfounderExtractor uses Perceiver-style cross-attention. By default, it operates at SENTENCE level. With confounder_hierarchical=true, it uses HIERARCHICAL attention: sentence-level sparse attention to focus on relevant sentences, then token-level attention within those sentences to preserve fine-grained signal.

architecture._comment_latents: Latent confounders: learnable query vectors that learn to attend to relevant sentences
architecture._comment_explicit: Explicit confounders: concept phrases encoded as additional query vectors. These provide inductive bias for known confounders.
architecture._comment_dimensions: value_dim is the dimension of each confounder representation AND the final output dimension
architecture._comment_hierarchical: HIERARCHICAL MODE: When true, uses per-sentence BERT encoding + token-level cross-attention. This preserves fine-grained token signal (e.g., 'PS is 0' vs 'PS is 2') that sentence embeddings may lose.
architecture._comment_token_encoder: HuggingFace model for token encoding in hierarchical mode. Options: distilbert-base-uncased (fast), emilyalsentzer/Bio_ClinicalBERT (clinical domain)
architecture._comment_max_sentence_tokens: Maximum tokens per sentence. Sentences longer than this are truncated.
architecture._comment_sentence_encoder: (For non-hierarchical mode only) SentenceTransformer model for encoding sentences.
architecture._comment_sentence_processing: max_sentences limits how many sentences to process per document. Sentences beyond this are dropped.
architecture._comment_attention: Cross-attention from latent confounders to sentence/token embeddings
architecture._comment_iterations: (For non-hierarchical mode only) Number of iterative refinement passes (Perceiver-IO style).
architecture._comment_self_attention: (For non-hierarchical mode only) Whether latent confounders attend to each other between passes.
architecture._comment_sparse: Sparse attention forces each confounder to focus on few sentences instead of spreading attention everywhere.
architecture._comment_sparse_methods: Options: 'entmax' (soft sparsity, recommended), 'topk' (hard top-k selection), 'softmax' (no sparsity)
architecture._comment_topk: Only used when sparse_method='topk'. Number of sentences each confounder can attend to.
